agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. Enforces accrual-based balance integrity, advance notice rules, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days an employee holds. Must never go negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which employees earn 1.5 leave days per month of active employment."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to use accrued leave for a specified date range, subject to advance notice rules and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A special category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 calendar days of the absence) when accompanied by a doctor's note, exempt from the standard 3-working-day advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days (typically Mon-Fri excluding public holidays) used to calculate the advance notice period for leave requests."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave days are transferred to the next year, capped at a maximum of 10 days. Days above 10 are forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days that exceed the 10-day carryover cap at year-end."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate that must accompany retroactively submitted medical leave to validate the absence."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Given an employee who has worked N complete months, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days (before any usage or forfeiture)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance must never go negative. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative balance means an employee consumed leave that does not exist — this is a data integrity violation representing a physical impossibility in accrual-based accounting.","testable_assertion":"At no point in time shall an employee's leave balance be less than zero. Any leave request that would reduce the balance below zero must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance notice period is a policy preference that management could shorten or lengthen without breaking system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before its start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retroactive window and documentation requirement are configurable policy decisions.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request for a past date is accepted only if (a) it is submitted within 5 days of the absence and (b) a doctor's note is attached. Otherwise it is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the employee's manager may approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Approval authority is an organizational policy; the company could delegate to team leads or auto-approve without corrupting data.","testable_assertion":"A leave request transitions to approved or rejected only via a manager action."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Deducting balance for leave that was not granted would corrupt the accounting ledger — balance must reflect only approved absences.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request is rejected, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from before the request."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Granting leave beyond contract termination is a logical impossibility — the employment relationship no longer exists.","testable_assertion":"Any leave request with an end date after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The carryover cap is a policy threshold that leadership could raise or lower.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, an employee's carried-over balance equals min(previous_year_remaining_balance, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 complete months with no leave taken","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient balance'"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request starting 2 working days from now","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient advance notice — minimum 3 working days required'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 2 days starting 5 working days from now","then":"The request is accepted and enters 'pending approval' state"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent yesterday due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request today with a doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted and enters 'pending approval' state (retroactive within 5 days, note present)"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent 6 days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request today with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'retroactive medical leave must be submitted within 5 days of absence'"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'doctor's note required for retroactive medical leave'"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager rejects a pending leave request for 3 days","when":"The rejection is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance remains unchanged and the request status becomes 'rejected'"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A manager approves a pending leave request for 3 days","when":"The approval is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced by 3 days and the request status becomes 'approved'"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 14–16","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'leave request cannot extend beyond contract end date'"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 8 days and 0 days are forfeited"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request for exactly the contract end date (last day of contract)","expected_behavior":"Request is accepted if all other rules pass — the end date is inclusive of the last working day under contract."},{"scenario":"Medical leave spanning multiple days submitted retroactively — only some days fall within the 5-day window","expected_behavior":"The system should evaluate the 5-day window against the first day of the absence. If the first absence day is more than 5 days ago, reject the entire request."},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted exactly 3 working days in advance, accounting for weekends/holidays","expected_behavior":"The request meets the minimum notice requirement. The system must correctly skip non-working days when counting the 3-day notice period."},{"scenario":"Employee submits leave, then accrual runs mid-process before manager approves","expected_behavior":"Balance check must occur at approval time (or both submission and approval time). An employee should not have leave approved if their balance has since been consumed by another approved request."},{"scenario":"Year-end carryover when employee has a pending (unapproved) leave request","expected_behavior":"Pending requests should not reduce balance. Carryover is calculated on actual (committed) balance. If the pending request is later approved in the new year, it deducts from the carried-over balance."},{"scenario":"Employee's contract end date falls mid-leave (request submitted before contract end was set or changed)","expected_behavior":"System should re-validate existing pending requests when contract end date changes, and flag or auto-reject requests that now violate BR-7."},{"scenario":"Zero months worked — new employee on day one","expected_behavior":"Leave balance is 0. No leave can be requested until the first month accrual occurs."},{"scenario":"Medical leave submitted on exactly day 5 after absence","expected_behavior":"Accepted — the 5-day window is inclusive (within 5 days means days 0 through 5)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct accrual and forfeiture rules prevent overgranting of leave which directly impacts labor cost. Miscalculation of carryover could create hidden liabilities on the balance sheet. Proper rejection of invalid requests avoids unplanned staffing gaps.","operational":"Automated enforcement of advance notice, balance checks, and contract boundaries reduces manual HR intervention. Year-end carryover automation eliminates error-prone spreadsheet processes. Clear approval workflow ensures manager oversight without bottlenecks.","user":"Employees gain transparency into their accrual and balance. Medical leave retroactive submission provides flexibility for genuine illness. Clear rejection reasons reduce confusion and support tickets.","risk":"Negative balance bug would allow employees to take unearned leave — financial and fairness risk. Failure to enforce contract end date constraint could create legal exposure. Incorrect forfeiture could trigger employee disputes or labor law violations depending on jurisdiction.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Directly affected — accrual accuracy, request experience, balance visibility, and year-end forfeiture fairness."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Approval workflow efficiency; rely on system to pre-validate requests so they only make judgment calls, not accounting checks."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Reduced manual tracking, auditability of leave records, compliance with internal policies."},{"group":"Finance / Payroll","impact":"Accurate leave liability reporting, correct payroll deductions for unapproved absences."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Forfeiture rules and retroactive medical leave windows may be subject to labor law constraints depending on jurisdiction."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The spec is well-defined with clear, testable rules and no internal contradictions. All major business rules are explicitly stated. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified (see low-confidence areas), but none rise to the level of blocking ambiguity — they are implementation details that reasonable defaults can handle.","high_confidence_areas":["Accrual rate and balance integrity (BR-1, BR-2)","Advance notice rules and medical leave exception (BR-3, BR-4)","Rejection does not consume balance (BR-6)","Contract end date boundary (BR-7)","Year-end carryover cap and forfeiture (BR-8)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Definition of 'month worked' — is it calendar month, 30-day period, or prorated for partial months?","Whether the 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is calendar days or working days (assumed calendar)","When exactly balance is deducted — at submission, at approval, or at the start of leave","Whether public holidays are defined in-system or externally for working-day calculations","Jurisdiction-specific labor law constraints on forfeiture — some jurisdictions prohibit forfeiting accrued leave"],"assumptions":["'Month worked' means a complete calendar month of employment","The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is counted in calendar days","Leave balance is reserved at submission and confirmed/released at approval or rejection","Working days follow a standard Mon-Fri schedule with configurable public holidays","Accrual is calculated monthly, not daily"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

Enforces accrual-based balance integrity, advance notice rules, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days an employee holds. Must never go negative."},{"term":"Accru
